dashboard.subtitle: Welcome to your NN Auth System dashboard

landing.title: NN Auth System
landing.subtitle: Complete authentication system with registration, login, password change and recovery. Built to learn Spring Boot and React applying security best practices.
landing.featuresSubtitle: Each feature implements a real security practice

footer.credit: NN Auth System — SENA educational project

register.title: Secure registration
register.description: BCrypt password hashing. Strength validation: minimum 8 characters, uppercase, lowercase and numbers.

jwt.title: Stateless JWT
jwt.description: 15-minute access token + 7-day refresh token. The server stores no sessions — scales horizontally.

email.title: Email verification
email.description: User must verify email before login. Prevents registration with someone else's email.

recovery.title: Password recovery
recovery.description: Email-based reset with single-use token valid for 1 hour. Always identical response (anti-enumeration).

s1.heading: 1. System purpose
s1.p1: NN Auth System is an educational project developed within the SENA program. Its purpose is to demonstrate the implementation of a complete authentication system with Spring Boot (Java 21) and React. It is not intended for production use with real data.

s2.heading: 2. Acceptable use
s2.p1: Users agree to use the system exclusively for learning and testing purposes. Attempting to breach the security of the system, performing brute-force attacks, or using the service for activities that violate applicable laws is prohibited.

s3.heading: 3. Test data
s3.p1: As an educational project, we recommend not using real personal data. Use test emails and passwords. Stored data may be deleted at any time.

s4.heading: 4. Liability
s4.p1: The system is provided "as is" for educational purposes. No continuous availability is guaranteed and no liability is assumed for data loss.

s1.heading: Data we collect
s1.p1: When creating an account, we collect: full name, email address and password. The password is stored hashed with BCrypt — never in plain text.

s2.heading: How we use the data
s2.p1: Data is used exclusively for authentication within the system. It is not shared with third parties. This is an educational project — the data is for technical demonstration.

s3.heading: Browser storage
s3.p1: The system uses localStorage to store the refresh token (needed to maintain the session between page reloads) and the theme preference (light/dark).

s4.heading: Contact
s4.p1: For any privacy inquiries, visit our contact page.

s1.heading: Do we use cookies?
s1.p1: The system does not use HTTP cookies. Instead, it uses the browser's localStorage for the refresh token and theme preference.
s1.p2Technical: Technical note: localStorage is accessible only by JavaScript from the same origin. HTTP cookies can be marked as HttpOnly, making them inaccessible to JavaScript — more secure against XSS attacks. In a real production system, the refresh token should be stored in an HttpOnly cookie.
